title: Stillingia marginata
taxonomy: Stillingia marginata is classified within the plant family Euphorbiaceae. This family is commonly known as the spurge family, which includes over 2,000 species of flowering plants. The Euphorbiaceae family is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Stillingia marginata, like other members of the Euphorbiaceae family, has milky sap and produces small, greenish flowers. The exact classification of Stillingia marginata may also be expressed at the genus and species level within the plant kingdom.
uses: Stillingia marginata, also known as Yellowroot, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits. Its root has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's root contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. Additionally, it has been used as a treatment for liver and kidney issues in some traditional practices. Further research is needed to fully understand the efficacy and safety of using Stillingia marginata for medicinal purposes.

parts: Stillingia marginata is a plant species native to the southeastern United States. The medicinal parts of Stillingia marginata are primarily its roots, which contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and potential anti-cancer properties. intro: Stillingia marginata, also known as Queen's Root, is a perennial plant native to North America, belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, offering potential health benefits in treating various ailments, including pain, fever, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Stillingia marginata include stillingine, a unique alkaloid compound, as well as other secondary metabolites, such as flavonoids and lignans, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Stillingia marginata is commonly prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, and is often used to treat skin issues, such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
